lines.st: The man steps into the room. The woman in the wheelchair utters not a word.
He tidies up the notes and documents scattered across the table.
He swaps the withered flowers in the vase out for fresh ones.
She continues reading her newly borrowed book, as if she takes no notice of him.
It is silent.
Perhaps because all there is to say has long since been said.
Or perhaps because it is not yet time for that which shouldn't be said.
Without warning, the man speaks.

lines.x: She'll be back soon.
The Legati spent so long preparing for this meeting. Of course she will.
How long will you stay?
Until I finish what I must do.
For all I can accomplish here is the delivery of the Lateran people's souls.
I should go.
Don't come back.

lines.st: The man stands silent for a while, before turning and leaving the room.
The woman pushes her wheelchair up to the window.
She opens it, and looks out.
She's watched the sun rise and set over Laterano countless times through this window frame.
The city is as noisy as ever tonight, the Laterans being strangers to both tranquility and exhaustion.
So what of tomorrow on?

lines.x: Mamma, drink some hot water.
Good girl, *cough* *cough*... Mamma's alright.
Mamma... I'll read you a storybook.
Cecelia, my darling Cecelia.
Mamma really hates to leave you.
Are you going to leave me, Mamma?
No, *cough* *cough*... Mamma's never, ever wanted... to leave you...
She's just got no choice...
Where are you going, Mamma? Can I go with you too? Is that okay?
You can't go where I'm about to go, Cecelia... It's very cold, very lonely, and so, so empty.
I'll go with you, I'll be with you so you won't be lonely.
...I'm sorry, Cecelia.
Your childhood should have been so much more blessed...
I HAVE been blessed. With you, Mamma.
Cecelia... Cecelia. Listen now, listen.
I'm listening, Mamma.
I won't be able to look after you soon... Someone will come pick you up.
Go with him. He'll take you to Papà.
Papà will look after you for me, okay?
Can't you come with me? I bet Papà really wants to see you...
Mamma... has something important to do... So I can't go with you.
Once you see Papà, tell him that your Mamma really misses him too.
Also, tell him... I'm sorry. Okay?
Mamma, what are you sorry for?
Listen to me, listen to your Mamma, Cecelia!
I will, Mamma... Don't cry...
Cecelia, my poor Cecelia... Giving birth to you was the greatest blessing in my life. But what if you find it a lifelong curse... my child, my beloved daughter...
Who will bless you? Who can?
Cecelia, my darling Cecelia... Hide yourself. Don't let them find you...
Run away... leave Laterano...
The mother holds her daughter's hands. Through her eyes flash myriad emotions, until finally all strength fades from them.
And they slowly, gently fall shut.
Mamma...?
The Sankta girl does not know what's happened.
When she notices her mother's hands have turned cold, she clasps them together against her own chest, hoping to provide some warmth to her.
There's a knock on the door.
Executor Federico of the Laterano Notarial Hall, on official business. Please open the door.
Sir, your check-in at the guesthouse has been taken care of.
The rest of your day is completely free! Might not be a bad idea to have a look around. You seemed quite eager the whole way.
Hmm—
Lateran City has some very distinct architecture.
You see? Wasn't it worth the trip?
I suppose it was.
What's the schedule for tomorrow?
Your meeting with His Holiness the Apostle, our Pope, is all set, so I'll be returning to the Basilica now. I'll fetch you tomorrow when it's time to meet him.
Please enjoy your stay in Laterano during the conference. No need to worry about expenses; it is our honor to host VIPs from the world over.
The world over... *sigh* I sure don't see how gathering all these foreigners in one place is gonna get anything done.
Still, how could I refuse such a generous invitation from His Holiness? Might as well take advantage of his hospitality while I'm here.
Excellent, now if you'll excuse me. Feel free to take Laterano by storm.
The architecture here sure is interesting. Columbia uses pillars like these too, but there're subtle differences...
Hmm. Seeing as I'm here...
Hm? What are they up to over there?
All set up?
I'm good. How's your end?
Done. Ezell, have we dispersed everyone in the area?
All the citizens likely to be directly affected, yes. Those in the secondary effect range are confirmed to be notified as well.
Shall we get started then?
Mm-hm... Current time is 10:22, on March 15th, 1099. The following act of demolition is carried out with Reserve Executor Ezell of the Notarial Hall as witness.
Alright. Here goes, three—
(What're those three Sankta doing standing around that pillar?)
Two—
You three are the only ones around here. What gives?
One, fire!—Huh, where'd you come from?!
What?! What was that explosion?! ...Something's coming down!
Save me, save me! Eeeek—!
I have to... what? The pillar's falling a lot slower now...
Save—
Cram it.
Eeek—
Huff... huff...
Th-That scared the skin off of me! What kind of crazy place is Laterano?! Whoever you are, Liberi, thank you for rescuing me.
What's your name? Find me at the Columbian Provisional Association, and I'll return the favor.
She's called Shimmering Vigil.
Shimmering Vigil...? Lateran names sure do sound silly sometimes...
That's not my name.
Hi... you're okay, right?!
Thanks, you two. That could've been a disaster.
Thank you for your aid.

lines.n: The Liberi Formerly Known As Shimmering Vigil
lines.x: You're welcome.
Say, what's going on here though? Bombings in the middle of the day? Don't tell me you have extremists sneaking in?
Uh...
Something's been on my mind.
Go on.
Do you think this pillar is absolutely perfectly positioned, or no?
I would say so; it fully embodies Laterano's architectural aesthetics. So?
So... I can't help but wanna see what would happen if we blew it up.
Ahh—
I have to admit, it's a fine idea.
But the conference is starting soon, right? I heard there'll be a lotta people from afar. I think His Holiness even said we should show some restraint for a while.
He did.
However, he said 'show some restraint', not 'don't do anything'.
I get that His Holiness is hoping to give the visitors a good impression. But, my dear friend, what do you say to my humblest of opinions? I think a 'good impression' must be based on reality.
You couldn't be more correct, my friend.
So, we filed with the Notarial Hall, got our executor here to witness for us, and evacuated everyone nearby...
Before giving this beautiful pillar just the perfect blast...
Except, we never expected you to show up right at that moment.
We're so sorry, esteemed sir, for unwittingly doing you harm.
You... set off an explosion on a nomadic city street? And filed with the government... for an official witness?! Were you... you Laterans always this... uninhibited?

lines.n: The Liberi Formerly Known As Shimmering Vigil
lines.x: Of course we abide by the Commandments and all relevant regulations.

lines.n: The Liberi Formerly Known As Shimmering Vigil
lines.x: But if it's not explicitly forbidden, anything's fair game. And Lateran City values and protects every citizen's rights.
Don't worry, sir. Just your average everyday explosion, perfectly normal.
What if someone gets hurt...?
We know our limits. That said, I'd also like to extend my apologies to you for this accident.
Truly sorry, sir.
Maybe it hasn't quite sunk in for you yet, but believe me, stay long enough and you'll learn to love this city.
Er... I think I can live without that sinking in.
And there you have it.
Welcome to Laterano, sir.
Tut-tut, if it isn't Mostima. Just what is her ability? Is she slowing down time, or merely tricking people into perceiving it that way?
It blows my mind every time.
Oren, what's going on over there?
Not much, just a normal day in my lovely hometown.
Though I did get a better show than expected.
The others are waiting for us.
I know. Let's go.
The Legati.
A special Messenger division established under the auspices of the incumbent Pope.
Laterano had previously held a detached position, maintaining neutrality among the central powers.
As such, Lateran officials were sometimes tasked by foreign powers with relaying critical information between different parties in the course of diplomacy.
This role was normalized and institutionalized with the establishment of the Legatus system. They have become a special force moving between the nations.
With Laterano's absolute neutrality as a foundation, the Legati aim to promote the circulation of important information between countries.
1098: via the Legati, the Pope sends an invitation to all nations of Terra:
'Gather for a conference at Laterano. I invite every nation to dispatch its envoys for a discussion regarding our collective security.'
1099: the Legatus system celebrates its thirtieth anniversary.
In March of that year, Legati from all across Terra proceed for Laterano together with the envoys of numerous nations.
One of those two just now seemed a lot like a Pontifica Cohors, and the other... was that a Legatus?
A strange combination.
Ezell, are you gonna stick around now that your witness work's done?
Don't worry, we'll rebuild the pillar as good as new, just like we wrote in our application.
Good, I'll leave you to it then. I'll be going now, I've still got quite a bit on my plate.
Er. Just to confirm, I'm looking for a will registered by someone who's... passed away?
A typical single woman's residence, with no trace of any other occupants.
In the bedroom, her body lies serenely. No signs of poisoning, no signs of violence.
Federico here.
Female citizen's body discovered at 7-265 Via Thervatius, Pagus Stevonus.
Preliminary determination of the cause of death: natural. Please notify the Ecclesia Requietum of the pagus to come and inter the remains.
Preliminary search found no last will and testament. Please initiate new assignment: confirm deceased's identity, search for will according to any pre-registered information.
Federico out.
Mamma...
Don't go out.
Don't let anyone else find you.
Never, ever approach anyone in uniform.
That's what Mamma said.
Is the uniform man gone? Or still here?
It's alright. When someone visits, just take a nap on your little sofa, just like always. You can go back to Mamma when you wake up.
Don't panic... Cecelia. Why are you so worried?
Why can't you fall asleep?
It's okay, it's okay... you just need to wait for the stranger to leave.
It'll all be alright when you're with Mamma again.
Seems things went pretty well in Sargon, huh? They sent out a whole bunch of envoys.
Don't get me started. The pashas are all eager enough, but the problem is none of them can agree on anything.
I'm already thanking Heaven they haven't started brawling yet.
What about Kazimierz? Their military's sizeable, no matter how you slice it.
The Adeptus Sprawiedliwi turned down our proposal, but the K.G.C.C. was actually very interested.
One rep even sought me out.
We had a long chat... he understood the international situation as well as any Legatus.
He might even come in person, if he's not too busy.
But enough about us. How about you, Oren?
The situation in Victoria is anything but simple these days. I'm sure you've heard a thing or two.
Duke Gododdin and I get along fine, but... it's less that he doesn't want to appoint anyone to be here, and more that he CAN'T.
Everything's balanced on a knife's edge, more or less.
Yan didn't dispatch anyone either. Not that they didn't send a stream of gifts 'conveying our thanks to His Holiness'... How very Yanese of them.
Kjerag actually sent out a few, after rising to prominence these last two years... and every other nation's envoys are already here.
To be honest, I never imagined this job would go so smoothly.
Scared?
A little bit.
You haven't been a Legatus for too long, if I remember right.
Less than four years, I guess.
But our organization has been active for thirty years now.
After all our efforts, I'd say every last one of us has earned these results.
Besides, we're still a long way off from what we're hoping for—a true 'Summit of Nations', right?
We need more than envoys showing up in the Lateran Basilica audience hall. We need the people in power, the ones who can actually change things.
So save your nerves for the day we have to greet the big guns instead.
Thanks for putting it like that—now I've got no idea if I should be excited or terrified.
I see you can't stop yourself from terrorizing the newbies, Oren.
You wound me.
Nothing to get keyed up about, even if that day really comes.
That's what we're qualified for, aren't we?
Maybe so.
So, are the acting Legati of each region all present?
We're only missing Mostima.
Mostima? Ooh, I just happened to see her.
What's she doing?
Right now? Taking in her native air again.
Ah, forget her. We were never counting on her to come anyway.
She can report to His Holiness last and we're good, just like always.
Sanctitas advenit.
In a split second, the hall is silent and still.
All of the Messengers' gazes lie on one spot.
The teacher of all Laterano, their guide, their spiritual leader—
Good morning, children. Have you had breakfast yet?
Well?
No sign of external force. It was likely due to illness.
A pitiable soul. May she not ail so on the other side.
Dimming Sankta, your remains shall be conferred unto the Ecclesia Requietum, where you shall rest.
Here, this was hung on the bedroom wall. Hand it to a member of the Notarial Hall.
Why did they take Mamma away...?
What... do I do...?
Cecelia casts her gaze to the entrance.
You can't go out, you can't be found... or else you'll be in danger, Cecelia.
That's what Mamma said.
But if Mamma got taken away, will she be in danger too?
Fiammetta...
Wait, you're actually using my name for once? What gives?
Aw, you don't like it? More a fan of 'Shimmering Vigil', then? Or do you want a new one?
Fiammetta's fine, thanks. Seriously though, what is it?
Say... my lovely coworkers should be in the Lateran Basilica right about now, no? Reminiscing about me, maybe?
If you're aware, then maybe you should show your face for once.
Nah, forget it, I'll never get used to the ambience in there.
Besides, who'd want to see a living warning sign rock on in there? They'd just be uncomfortable.
Since when do you care if other people are uncomfortable?
I'm not so bored I'd make myself unwelcome just for fun.
Objection: case in point. I'll admit you're enjoying being back here a lot more than I expected, though.
Really?
'Welcome to Laterano' and all that.
Ah, I think I'm just in high spirits. Probably from coming home after so long.
Yeah right.
Hahah, sure, whatever, let's go.
Where to?
To see her, of course.
Oh, maybe she's in a different hospital now?
It's been ages since the last time you returned, after all.
Follow me.
I haven't been to this pagus too often. It seems not too far from the outskirts... I should be nearly there, right? 7-265, Via Thervatius...
Is that an... Ecclesia Requietum hearse stopped by that door?
Hello? Excuse me, Sister!
Calm down, young executor.
Have you finished the inspection already?
Correct. There was a Sankta who departed us by illness.
May I quickly verify the remains?
Confirmation complete. The deceased is the homeowner Feoria La Porta.
My assignment mentioned no will was discovered nearby. May I ask if you noticed anything resembling one during your inspection?
We did not. However, this is for you... the Notarial Hall would wish to reclaim it, correct?
Is this... her patron firearm? Understood, I'll take it back with me. Thank you both.
If you're done with the identity check and arms reclamation, please allow us to convey her to the Ecclesia Requietum.
Ah, very well. Thank you for your time.
Identity of the deceased confirmed. If I want to find any pre-registration of a will through the Notarial Hall system... Right, I can do a long-ranged search from my terminal...
Census addresses... homeowners...
Er, she didn't prepare a will?
The diagnosis record states...
Ah, given the illness, giving up on hospital treatment makes sense... but why wouldn't she leave a will...?
Hm...? Is that a neighbor's child? Why is she hiding from me...?
Watch out!
Wha—did she faint? But why? She only tripped...
Does anyone know who this child's parents are?! Does she live nearby?
I've never seen her before. Is she passed out...? What if she bumped her head when she fell?
I've never seen her either. Maybe she just moved in? I can ask around at the neighborhood hall.
What for?! If she's hit her head, then we need to get her to the hospital!
You hurry and take her there, executor. We live just around here. If her parents come looking, we'll point them straight to the hospital to find you.
Perfect, thank you all!
Grr... I didn't think Cecelia would run straight into a Notarial right outside her home...
What now, Patia?
What else? Follow them, make sure she's safe... and get her out of the Notarial Hall's hands!
Yucatan... doesn't Lateran food bug you at least, like, a little...?
Tastes here definitely lean on the sugary side, compared to Kjerag.
Don't you like sweet, though, Rus?
That doesn't mean I can take everything sweet!
*Sigh* I'm starting to miss Kjerag now.
It's that bitch Ratatos's fault, all blah blah 'grab every opportunity to reestablish our clan's status'... and she didn’t even come herself, throwing me under the sleigh out here.
It's a show of the Matriarch's trust in you. After that incident put us on the brink, it was your joint efforts with her that ensured the Browntails held on.
And you got tossed off to the side. You just love living behind the scenes, don't you?
Personally speaking, getting to see you shine is more than enough satisfaction for me, Rus.
Ooh, when did you learn to talk so smooth?
That aside, Madame, this conference may turn out more fruitful than we expected.
We didn't think so many countries would be here. It seems we might've underestimated Laterano's influence.
Yeah, that does sound about right... but don't you change the subject!
(Leithanian) Ugh, do you not have eyes?!
(Leithanian) You bumped into me, I think.
Your Leithanian is an embarrassment. What corner of the hinterland did you scurry out of?
I come from Kjerag, and it's certainly not the 'hinterland.'
Kjerag?
What kind of place is Kjerag?
Lady Sharon, Kjerag is the nation of the Snow Realm in the buffer zone between Victoria, Columbia, and Kazimierz.
There's a country in those mountains? Hah, the very definition of hinterland!
You think an envoy of a trifling glacial state has the standing to be flippant with me, a duchess?
Madame, first, I have to stress again, Kjerag isn't some 'hinterland' like you keep saying. You can probably thank your own narrow mind that you've never heard of my country.
Second, you're the one who bumped into me. You owe me an apology.
You dare!
Lady Sharon, we're in the Basilica...
Lady Sharon, permit I remind you, you currently stand in Laterano, and not your Leithanian domain.
And who are you?
A Lateran Legatus.
What are you trying to tell me, Legatus? This is the Basilica? What, do you intend to declare that as the host, Laterano sides with this provincial creature?
Laterano sides with order. Therefore, Lady Sharon, you should apologize to Madame Sciurus.
You know my name?
You'd expect me to know the names of all the diplomatic envoys, wouldn't you?
Oh? Then you must be well aware of who I am.
Of course, Duchess Francis of Leithanien.
But permit I remind you again, this is Laterano, Duchess.
In Laterano, you are asked to honor the Commandments and orderly abide the rule of law. No matter what privileges you enjoy in Leithanien, they do not exist nor apply in Laterano.
This is how Laterano treats its guests?
If you're willing to be a 'guest of Laterano,' Laterano will wholeheartedly treat you in kind.
—Oren, when did you start standing for Laterano, huh?
As a Lateran Legatus, do I not have the right?
Not in the Basilica.
Lady Sharon, please quell your anger.
Hmph. Don't you think it's a little late to be pacifying me, cardinal?
Your Grace, I beg your understanding. The Legatus who contradicted you will be given an appropriate punishment. And rest assured of the Lateran Curia's goodwill during your stay in the guesthouse.

Madame Sciurus, I beg your pardon as well. This fault for this minor misunderstanding lies with our shortcomings. If you would be willing to magnanimously accept my apologies, I hope to invite you in a short while to the side rooms for a talk.
Sciurus looks at Yucatan, and Yucatan gives a subtle nod.
Hah, Laterans. My, my.
Alessoe, Mattina, please look after these three most important guests in my stead.
Oren, come with me.
Here comes the punishment. Alright, alright...
Yes, the deceased in Pagus Stevonus did not leave behind a will nor any prior registration of one. I'll return to the Notarial Hall in a bit and submit for double-checking.
Currently? I'm at Pagus Stevonus Central Hospital... No, no, I'm alright, I just found a girl injured in a fall, I'm taking her to be examined right now...
No, I wasn't in a traffic accident...
No, the parents aren't with her. She's still unconscious, and for the time being we can't confirm her family circumstances. Once she wakes up, I'll bring her back to her guardians.
Right, and if she doesn't wake up soon, I might need to use Notarial Hall privileges to...
...Pardon? I can consider this one of my practical assignments...? Alright, I'll fill in all the documents after it's over.
Ah... sorry for that, I'm finished.
It's alright! The doc's physical checkup is done. No wounds anywhere, nothing on her head, so it probably wasn't a knock on the brain.
But the girl does have a small fever. Odds are she was ill. She'll still need a few more tests before we're sure what caused her to pass out, but it won't be anything too bad.
Doc's set her up in Ward 2127. You can wait there for the examination results.
Is she your sister? Are you on duty? Why are you bringing your little sister on an executor shift? I barely ever see executors here in Stevonus!
She's not my sister.
So why did you bring her here? Is she your target? Oh, Heavens, are executors really that ruthless? You'd execute a little girl like that? What did she do?
I only came across her in the street. She's not my target. She just lives in the vicinity, in all likelihood.
Really? She must be new around here, then.

This is the only hospital in Stevonus, so all the kids come here for checkups when they're sick... you should see the sight of the consulting room at Pediatrics whenever the seasons change. It's exhausting!
Lucky I'm on shift at Recovery next week. Have you heard of our Recovery department? It's famous!

The wheelchair rapid-fire competition last year there, oh, heavens me, I've never seen guns blaze that fast before, I still suspect that must've been a Gun-Knight... a little bit young for it, though.
What's that look for? Didn't I tell you Recovery was famous? We have multiple people from the Basilica all recuperating here!
Did you just say... the girl I brought here was newly moved into Stevonus?
I've never seen her before, that's why. If she's not your sister, then a kid that small can't have come scampering from another pagus, can she?
Well, obviously, there's the possibility I've just never seen her by pure chance. But I can tell a girl like her falls ill day in and day out... I've seen too many of them!
Where was I? Consulting in Pediatrics? Listen, last week...
Here you are gossiping with the family again, Elisa. The doctor wants you at the test center to pick up the results.
On it—
You're sure you fetched the right report?
Yyyes. Is there a problem?
I've never seen a blood analysis this strange before.
Once it's photocopied, send the original to the head doctor's office, along with this note from me.
I need to pay a visit to Recovery.
Hmph, that attendant's headed for the head doctor's office. So what now, Patia?
Allocate two to hold the report-deliverer back. Don't go big. Just hold things off, win a little time, and we're fine.
What about that doctor?
It looks like he's gone for Recovery. But not the end Cecelia's in.
Hm? That works. This way, the executor'll still be in the dark for now.
Everyone else, with me. We'll draw the executor away, and get Cecelia out before the hospital's people reach her.
Remember, move quick, but don't be rough with Cecelia.
She's our companion.
Don't make her hate us.

lines.st: Mamma, why do we need to go so far every time we meet Daddy? It's really dark now...
Because... Daddy's not able to come to Lateran City. He can't live with us.
Why?
Because he's different from us.
Then can't we stay with Daddy?
Daddy wouldn't want you to stay with him, Cecelia. You'll understand when you're older...

Why...? Does Daddy not like me? Is it because I'm not good enough?
If I'm a good girl, can Daddy be with us all the time?
Cecelia... you have to trust Daddy. He loves you, even if he can't be by our side.

lines.x: Unnh... nnh....
Mamma...
You're awake!
Who... Where am I...?
You're in the hospital, Miss. You fainted in the street, and I took you here since I couldn't find your parents.
Hospital... is this a hospital?
No... you—you're wearing a uniform, Mamma said...
Has your mama been telling you things?
(Come to think of it, when I first saw her, wasn't she hiding from me...?)
It's alright, Miss, I can take you to find your mama. I'm a good guy.
You can help me find Mamma? Ma—Mamma got taken away by people in gowns!
(This might just be a kidnapping case! Is that why she was all alone there?)
Okay, little Miss, stay calm. I'm a Notarial Hall executor. I'll help you find your mama. How did she get taken away?
You're a Notarial Hall...
That's right, I'm from the Notarial Hall. My name's Ezell. Look, here's my name tag.
Will you really help me find Mamma?
Of course. What's your name?
...I'm Cecelia.
...Nngh...
Huh?! What's wrong with... your halo...?
(What is this about...? Even in the most downcast moods, Sankta halos don't dim like this.)
(Unless...)
(But a child this small wouldn't even have a patron firearm yet. What could explain this...?)
...Nnnh...
(No. Beyond that...)
Cecelia, how do you feel? Is your halo...?
Sometimes... it goes like this... Mamma says I'm ill... it'll be fine soon...
Ezell has realized something.
Amidst her current pain, Cecelia is anxious about her missing mother too.
But he cannot sense any of her pain, nor anxiety, relayed from her.
Cecelia... is without a doubt a Sankta.
How could this be possible?
Cecelia, are you...
Are you this girl's relative?
Functionally.
Come with me. She's got somewhat special circumstances, and the doctor said he needs to talk with you in private.
There was a strong gust of wind just now. I'll close the window, in case she'd catch a cold.
Hm? Of course.
Alright. Cecelia, just get some rest, I'll be right back.
Don't worry, Cecelia. We'll keep you company.
The rooftop.
You said Cecelia's circumstances were special. Special how?
Well... we can't be exactly sure yet. We'll need to wait for the diagnosis. The doctor will be here shortly. Just hold on, and we'll see what he has to say.
I've already reported Cecelia's situation to the Notarial Hall. My colleagues are fast on their way.
What?!
Tell me who you are.
I'm an attendant, right? What are you talking about? The doctor will be here momentarily.
Would a normal hospital attendant get nervous hearing the words 'Notarial Hall?'
So you suckered me... I only needed to keep you here for a short while anyway. If I were you, I wouldn't be giving myself trouble.
Patia said to be more gentle with Cecelia... but not with you.
'Patia,' was it?
You'd do well to forget that one.
Come on out. Let's give our Notarial Hall man some break time.
So you're able to move around without your wheelchair now?
Yes, I've been rehabilitating pretty well.
Last year, they had a wheelchair rapid-firing contest, which I won... and then the doctors banned me from competing again.
We'd have to look for a Gun-Knight to beat your firing skills, I think.
Aww, that's nice to hear.
I'd be even happier if SOMEONE learned how to say a few nice words to the sick, like my darling Fiammetta here.
Mostima, are you just going to stand there pretending you're the curtains?
You know I'm not the type, Lemuen.
What type? This is your first time back in two or three years. My memory's not that good.
Fine. Lemuen fires better than anyone I know. Her skills are incredible.
Mm-hm, that's more like it.
Is El doing alright in Lungmen?
I saw her recently. She's doing great.
You'd better worry about yourself before her.
And I'll copy those words right back at you.
How long are you going to stay this time?
Dunno, really. But it'll be at least until the conference's over, Wednesday after next, I think?
Tuesday after.
Oh, Tuesday. Otherwise, it's up to how that old weirdo feels. Who knows if he'll send me on some weird and wacky assignment again.
Did you just call His Holiness...?
He's not gonna mind, Fiammetta.
If you want to stay for a bit longer, I could help you there.
That so.
Did you figure it out already?
Figure what out? What are you two talking about?
Did you really not realize, Fiammetta?
Who we've been handing our reports to for the last half year?
Was it that obvious?

If a someone's views are so easy to change, then they can't be worth that much to begin with.
Get out of the way.
I know you're only here to stall me.
But you know you can't hold me.
We both have work to do.
Your companions are... practically no different from any civilian. Do you want them paying the price for you?
You don't, Patia. So get out of the way, and save us both the effort.
We're home, Cecelia.
Um... I don't have the key, I can't get in... Mister Ezell, can you...
Are you really okay? Do you feel bad...? You've been looking down for a while.
I'm fine. Let me get the door open...
Why did the lady just now say you actually knew our address?
Cecelia, do you recognize this firearm?
Is this—Mamma's guardian gun?
Mister Ezell... why do you have Mamma's gun...?
I'm sorry, Cecelia...
Sorry for what...?
I don't really understand.
You... You know where Mamma is, don't you?
Yes, I do... I'm sorry, Cecelia.
I'm sorry... I don't know why I'm apologizing, either... I'm sorry.
Cecelia—your mother—passed away.
She's not here anymore.
If she's not here, where is she then? What does... 'passed away' mean?
I remember, this morning, Mamma said she was going somewhere, and she couldn't take me with her... Did you go there with her, Mister Ezell?
Can you... take me there too? Just once, just for a little bit?
I—I won't be selfish, and I'll listen to Mamma, and I won't stay there and say I won't leave... so... so...
Can you let me see Mamma one more time?
I haven't said goodbye to her yet.
Uh, what's happening? Why is she crying, Ezell? Just what did you do to a poor little girl?
Who are you? Hold on, that uniform... You're a Legatus?
Very sharp eyes, Senior Ezell.
By order of His Holiness the Pope, I'm here to take Cecelia to the Basilica.
Whoops, forgot to introduce myself. My name's Oren Argiolas. If you want to check my identity, no harm in using your regulation terminal from the Hall there.
Oh, no, forgot that too. You turned it off, didn't you?
How did you find this place, then?
Senior Ezell, you underestimate a Legatus. You thought your movements would be hard to track? Or did you think you were so thorough, you left zero trace along the way?
Your Excellency... If you're going to take Cecelia away, can I at least ask you a few questions first?
Hahah, you're funny, senior. You think I have to answer what you ask?
But I'm not such a cruel man... so shoot.
Just who is Cecelia?
Was she... born a fallen angel?
Colorful imagination, Ezell.
But it's not as mysterious as you think.
Cecelia's mixed-blood, that's all.
What? That's not possible... A Sankta's mixed-blood child can't be a Sankta!
There are very few cases of Sankta mixing blood with other races... but it's not rare enough to alarm the Lateran Curia...
You know the reason perfectly well, then.
Offspring of Sankta and other races don't become Sankta.
A Sankta and Feline's child turns out Feline, a Sankta and Forte's child turns out Forte.
Mixed Sankta offspring don't get their halo and wings of light when they say their first word, and they can't empathetically sense other Sankta.
To put it simply, they won't have any of the Sankta's distinguishing features. Without exception...
That's the generally accepted wisdom, anyway.
Cecelia is an exception.
She's a Sankta who shouldn't have turned out as one.
My second question is, should Cecelia is taken to the Basilica, how the Lateran Curia will... treat her?
I don't know.
Don't give me that look, Ezell. If I was going to lie to you, I'd feed you a much kinder one, no problem.
But the reality's what you see. Cecelia is an 'exception.' I couldn't tell you how the Pope is going to handle this.
But this Holy Father of ours... I don't know what impression you have of him, but, well.
At least, as I'm given to understand, he's not known to use extreme methods.
And look at how cute Cecelia is. How hardhearted would someone have to be to harm her, right?
Third question.
I cannot entirely trust your answers... I wish to accompany Cecelia to the Basilica.
Um, is that a question?
Sorry, but that's the one 'question' you'll have to forgive my answer on...
Oren, what are you doing here?
Oh, Fiammetta... Hm? Seems like you're in a bit of a mess.
None of your business.
No need to get worked up. Care to sit down for a cup of tea?
Enough of your jokes. Why are you here? Mostima never mentioned you were our support.
Well, Velliv didn't give me an official order either.
And personally, I'm more than willing to help, yeah?
Mostima says there's another group after the little girl, and you've already run into them. Is that right?
More or less.
After I heard from the Notarial Hall that Ezell cut his signal, I figured you might get intercepted. If you were to get caught out by the enemy, we'd end up losing the girl, no?
I don't know why Mostima isn't here to help you, but if she's pinned down, I figure I'll be doing her a favor.
After all, it's not every day I get the opportunity to have the two of you in my debt.
A few hopeless stragglers can't stop me.
'A cautious crew sails for ten thousand years,' Fiammetta. Velliv's going to have a hold over you at this rate.
Oh, that's a Yanese proverb. In other words, safety is born of caution.
Stop trying to show off, Oren. You're so annoying when you talk like that.
I'm not kidding. I at least got to express my goodwill. The rest is up to you.
Or, do you perhaps need me to escort you, my sweet Fiammetta?
No need. Goodbye, Oren.
Executor Ezell, hand over the girl.
It is in your best interest to comply.
Your name is Fiammetta, right? Seniora Fiammetta, I have a request.
Let's hear it then.
No sudden movements. There's no telling how I might react.
I know it was suspicious of me to go against Richele's orders and turn off my terminal, but...
Believe me, I haven't betrayed Laterano.
The reason I didn't follow the Hall's exact orders was because I didn't want anything to happen to Cecelia... And, I made a promise to her.
And how is that my problem?
Please just let me accompany you to the Basilica with Cecelia. If the Lateran Curia wants an explanation for this situation, wouldn't it be more convenient to have me around?
You're getting ahead of yourself. If you think you'll be able to influence how the Curia will handle this situation–– You'll want to disabuse yourself of that.
Going one step further... If you're thinking you could take the girl away if you don't like what the Lateran Curia plans to do with her...
Not gonna happen. Not in your wildest dreams.
I wouldn't dare. I'm well aware of the Basilica's Gun-Knights.
I am a Lateran executor, after all. I was concerned before because I didn't know what was happening with Cecelia, or exactly who was after her...
Now that I understand, I will comply with your arrangements.
Alright.
Um... Your name is Cecelia? Can you walk?
Ezell...
Cecelia, I can carry you, if you're too tired.
I'll carry her. You're too slow.
(*whispers*) Mister Ezell, I...
Don't worry. Everything will be okay.
Executor Ezell Pastore went to 7-265 Via Thervatius in Pagus Stevonus this morning to verify an identity, record a will, and retrieve a patron firearm.
Around noon, Ezell appeared in Stevonus Central Hospital, taking in an eight-year-old Sankta girl and departing in an unusual manner.
The resident of 7-265 Via Thervatius, Pagus Stevonus, Feoria La Porta, died of illness this morning and the body was moved to the Ecclesia Requietum.
Only one individual is registered to 7-265 Via Thervatius, and the census pagus also records only one individual residing there.

An investigation of twenty years of maintenance records at 7-265 Via Thervatius revealed that a basement had been added seven years ago, with contractor documents indicating that the space would be for storage.
This is a file photo of Feoria La Porta and surveillance footage of the girl that Ezell took to the hospital.
You must've already reached a conclusion about where the girl came from.

Additionally, according to the report submitted by Ezell this morning, Feoria La Porta did not leave a will or an advance directive. The reason is quite clear: she hoped to avoid contact with the Notarial Hall.
It is reasonable to infer that Feoria La Porta knew her time was short and sought to secure a future for her daughter.
It is currently impossible to determine whether Ezell is the object of Feoria La Porta's entrustment, but based on Ezell's background, the probability is low.
Combined with the information sent back by Fiammetta, the more likely trustee should be...
Astray.
Mostima, is this the reason you stayed behind in the Basilica?
Just temporarily.
Don't be so uptight, Vel. Cream puff?
Phew. Luckily, we went for the more conservative option.
It would've been awkward if Fiammetta walked in on me getting rid of the Notarial Hall's men.
Enough of your excuses. I'd avoid saying too much to that executor, if I were you. Cecelia's already on the Guide's side.
That's just one executor, Patia. Things haven't gotten so out of hand that I need to get personally involved.
Plus, I think that executor's concerns are simple enough. He found the pity within him to cause his heart to waver, and couldn't bear to see a sweet little girl suffer.
Now that we understand him, it's only a matter of time until he comes over, no violence necessary.
If only Fiammetta hadn't shown up so soon...
That is to say it's your fault for not stopping Fiammetta, Patia.
Just kidding.
But, I do take pity on that one's luck. What was a little old thing is now very complicated... That's what happens when you get ahead of yourself.
Is this, too, a trial given to us by the great and mighty Lateran?
Are you a believer again?
A true believer wouldn't say something like that. Those sorts, who treat hardships as trials, they turn it all into... a blessing from fate?
There are quite a lot of believers like that around the two of you, aren't there?
I'd take them over self-serving Sankta like you any day.
So, what's the plan now? Once Cecelia gets to the Basilica, is she going to come out again? Do we have some way to take her?
One step at a time, Patia. If we're talking about not wanting Cecelia to step foot in the Basilica, I'm even less inclined than you.
But, we haven't played all our cards, have we?
Go tail Fiammetta, but not too closely.
What about you?
I've got plenty on my plate. Just found out that someone's looking for me too. Popularity isn't all it's made out to be, you know.
Don't go inviting trouble for me.
Alright, looks like the next clue of the chain is clear. What do you plan to do?
I will go to him.
Even though I have no doubts about your, uh, strength... you're dealing with a well-connected Legatus. It'll look pretty bad if you make something out of nothing.
He is involved in this incident.
We have to look into this precisely because he's involved, right? Sometimes, I envy the way you see things.
But what if he doesn't know anything?
I need to investigate to be clear.
That's true. In any case, the fact that a Legatus is involved at all warrants looking into.
What I didn't expect is that Feoria, an ordinary and reclusive Sankta, would provide us with so many leads. An undocumented girl, a Legatus...
And... a Sarkaz.
Hmm, you're leaving? Not even going to say goodbye?
Well, alright then...
Now that you're gone...
Don't blame me, Oren. I can't stop Federico.
I'll put in a report for you. That's about all I can do.
She's asleep.
She's exhausted.
You mentioned that you made her a promise. What was it?
That I'd help her find her mother.
Her mother? Wait, didn't Mostima say in the message that her mother is...
Yes, dead. Saw the body with my own eyes in the back of the Ecclesia Requietum's hearse.
Then, why'd you promise her that?
I didn't know, at the time.
And then? Did you tell her?
I did. But at her age, I'm not sure she understands the concepts of 'passing on' or 'death.'
Must've been tough for you.
And... don't look so upset. I don't think the Pope... will treat the girl too harshly.
Her name is Cecelia.
Seniora Fiammetta...
Just Fiammetta is fine.
Miss Fiammetta... I was thinking, after Cecelia goes to the Basilica, will she ever come out again...?
I can't promise you anything. But the way I see things, all else aside, she has a halo. That makes her a Sankta.
And when dealing with a Sankta, all must obey the Commandments, be it the Lateran Curia or the Notarial Hall. At least that's what I believe.
Even if she's undocumented.
Uhh... is that really the main issue?
Who knows? Might be possible to get her registered retroactively.
But, there's... something wrong with Cecelia's halo.
I'm not entirely sure how serious this whole mixed-blood Sankta issue gets... But at least the Legatus implied that Cecelia would be fine.
A lot of foreign envoys have been gathering in Laterano lately, right?
His Holiness has been preparing a major summit... I can't help but worry that Cecelia will be a target for someone trying to stir up trouble.
Sounds like you've really thought this one through.
Coming from the other angle, what would the Lateran Curia do to prevent that from happening?
Miss Fiammetta, I don't know if I should even be thinking about these things.
But I can't just not think.
You'll have to pardon me for being blunt.
But what's the point of worrying about all this? Is there any part of it you can actually control?
If you know that Cecelia is in danger, then you should also understand that for Cecelia, the Basilica is the safest place.
You're right.
Nngh...
Sorry, I fell asleep...
Did we wake you?
N-No, I just...
Ezell, is that your stomach growling?
Want me to buy you a donut, Cecelia?
D-Donut? Okay! Thank you, Mister Ezell...
Don't worry, I'll go for it. I need a break, my arms are sore from carrying her around.
(*whispers*) There's no way I can handle her long-term, alright?
But don't do anything stupid, Ezell. I've got my eye on you.
You still don't trust me?
Oh, and turn on your terminal. You're not allowed to have it off.
Yeah, I know.
(*whispers*) Mister Ezell, that lady was so mean to you before... I'm scared.
Don't worry, Cecelia. She's not a bad person.
Mister Ezell... can't I go see Mamma? Can't I even say goodbye to her?
Um... Well, we need to take you somewhere else first, so you can be safe.
As for your mama... I'll tell you about her later. Let me... think about it first. Is that okay, Cecelia?
Um...
Do you hear that?
Hear what?
Someone is singing...
Let's see... Oh, you're right! It seems to be coming from that cotton candy cart... But I'm surprised you could hear it with all the noise on the street. You have really good hearing, Cecelia.
It's because I recognize that song!
Mamma taught it to me!
Um... I can't go off on my own, right?
It's okay. We can't go over there, but I can get the cotton candy cart to come over here.
Excuse me––
Is someone calling us?
Hum hum hum~♪
You really like that song, don't you...? Hey, there really is someone hailing us. Let's head over there. If we can't get any info, let's at least make some money.
All~ righty~♪
Hello...
Oh hello, little girl, would you like some cotton candy?
Yes...
We'll get two, or actually, three sticks. This flavor here, this one here, and then the swirl. Is that alright?
No problem.
Three sticks, coming right up.
Um, excuse me... What's the name of that song you were singing earlier?
Mamma taught me the same song, but never told me what it's called...
Huh? Your mama... taught you this song?
Um, excuse me, the cotton candy! The cotton candy is coming out!
Ahh, I'm so very sorry, just give me a moment––
Hold on, the compartment's about to overflow! Let me help you...
Sorry, sorry!
(*whispers*) Your hat!!! Your hat's caught!
*rip*––
W-Wait, don't get the wrong idea, she's a Caprinae...
Miss!
Your horns look just like Papà's...
Are you also a Sarkaz?
(*whispers*) Put it back on!!
I, no, I'm... Little missy, you, you've got it all wrong...
A Sarkaz infiltrating Laterano... Are you also after Cecelia?
What happened, Ezell?
Miss Fiammetta, this particular individual... is a Sarkaz.
But there's another sentence that I dare not say.
Tch... Guess we have no choice, now that it's come to this...
You've got guts, I'll give you that...
*pant*...*pant*....
They're trying to run!
Not on my watch!
Urgh... I'll cover you, run!
Interesting, a Perro blocking an attack for a Sarkaz... Neither of you is getting away.
Ezell, you stay put. Don't you even think about––
No funny business, I know. The terminal is on, Miss Fiammetta.
Trust me, all I care about is ensuring Cecelia's safety.
Change of plans. You can't stay here. Take Cecelia to the Basilica right now.
It's more dangerous for us to stay in the open like this.
Let's go, Cecelia.
Huh? Weren't we going to the big statue? This is the wrong way.
We're taking a little detour.
What's a detour?
It means we're going somewhere else right now...
The Ecclesia Requietum.

lines.st: A Sarkaz.
I planned to ask Miss Fiammetta for a favor after we reached the Basilica.
To have a high-ranking executor look after her, or the Pontifica Cohors to protect her...
And confirm that Cecelia poses no threat to Laterano...
To let Cecelia see her mother Feoria one more time before she's interred.
But, a Sarkaz.
If Cecelia's father is a Sarkaz... then she should also be a Sarkaz.
And yet she still has a halo...
...I might be dreaming.
If Cecelia was nothing more than another 'exception'...
But she is a Sarkaz...
How could a Sarkaz with a halo be allowed to leave the Basilica?
Even if... even if the worst doesn't befall her...
How would she ever be allowed to leave?
Even if this gets me expelled from the Notarial Hall, I have to try.
I must bring Cecelia to the Ecclesia Requietum.
And at least let her say goodbye.

lines.x: You can't run.
She's gaining on us...
What do we do? What do we do? We can't shake her.
This is all my fault... That Liberi's not going to let us get away. Run! I'll hold her off!
But...
Hurry!
Stand back.
An...do...ain...
You're not getting away again.
SHOW YOURSELF RIGHT NOW!
I truly cannot understand why His Holiness would adopt such a... hands-off attitude towards the Astray.
Can you tell me, Mostima, since the two of you are well-acquainted?
Not gonna talk about that with you.
That's awfully petty of you.
I managed to keep him from killing me, only for you to very nearly do me in.
Hard to forget something like that.
And that's the reason you always wear a hat in front of me? Doesn't it get uncomfortable?
I've been a Legata for this long already. It's just a hat.
And as for you, you should probably let sleeping beasts lie.
Do you resent me, Mostima?
Laterano has always needed the Lateran Curia to keep things going, and that's always rubbed some people the wrong way. So I don't really care.
I think we have a little misunderstanding. That few fallen angels remain in the Church has nothing to do with the Church itself, but rather the Sankta nature of those fallen angels.
My attitude may be a bit peculiar, but... do you not deem yourself an aberration?
Considering the way you're talking to me, aren't you afraid I'll become one of those 'regular fallen angels'?
If that was a possibility, I wouldn't be 'talking to you that way' in the first place.
Fine. Let's talk business first. When's Fiammetta getting transferred?
Nothing good's gonna come out of her sticking to me.
Shouldn't you be asking her instead?
Brought it up before. No good.
And you think I'd have a better shot?
Looks to me like the convenient part of power is you get to ignore what other people want.
There's a price to be paid for that... So let's hear it. Why do you want Fiammetta transferred?
She said she was my escort. Are you guys not curious as to what needs escorting?
Of course, there are some who think she's a warden or jailer to a fallen angel like me.
And they don't know the first thing about Fiammetta.
I'm just the bait she's using to get at Andoian.
I'm sure she'd be terribly hurt if she heard you say that.
Alright, I'll try saying it another way... the bait is the Lock and Key I'm carrying on my back.
And? What's wrong with letting Fiammetta do as she pleases? If she can get rid of Andoain or even capture him and bring him back to the Basilica, I'd be very pleased.
Or are you saying that even the two of you together can't catch Andoain?
Not necessarily. As long as Andoain is still fixated on the Lock and Key, he can't escape.
But... let me think.
What if Andoain has already found another path?
What really sucks is I've moved on. Lemuen has moved on. Maybe even Andoain has moved on... But Fiammetta is still trapped in that rainy night eight years ago.
There's no way I could bring that up with Fiammetta.
And it's times like these where I start to miss that good old empathy...
Oh wait, Fiammetta is a Liberi anyway. Wouldn't do me any good.
This conversation sure brings me back.
What, are you here to share the wisdom of one who has walked that path?
If you'll allow me to exercise my authority as an elder.
By all means.
Do you remember what you said to me eight years ago?
I don't want to ruin the moment we're having, but...
Sorry, Your Holiness. I can't say that I do.
It was, 'No... I still have things to do, and people to protect.'
Velliv's impression of me is making my skin crawl.
You weren't willing to let me choose for you. So why, now, do you need to choose for Fiammetta?
Each must find her own path. She must find it, and then traverse it.
Vel, this is my same answer to you.
We're almost there, Cecelia.
I'm sorry I'm so slow, Mister Ezell... The sun is about to go behind the mountain.
You're doing a great job! Even though we can't use public transit, we're almost off the plate. If I were as strong as Miss Fiammetta, you wouldn't have to work so hard.
It's okay! Actually, Mamma took me out once and we went somewhere far, far away from home... just like here.
But, won't Miss Fiammetta find out that we sneaked out here?
If she's only looking for my terminal's signal... then hopefully that will buy me a little bit of time.
The important thing is that we're here.
Is this where Mamma went?
Cecelia... I've been thinking about how to explain this to you for a long time.
Feoria, your mama... is here. But she's also not.
I have no way of letting you see your mama again, but at least you'll be able to say goodbye to her here.
Mister Ezell... I don't think I get... what you're saying...
If she's here, but she's also not, does that mean... she went somewhere else?
Where did she go?
Let's head in first.
Before my father passed away, he always hoped there would be nothing but smiles at his funeral. That we'd see no tears. But, I...
If I may, I believe what your esteemed father truly wished was for you not to fall into grief over his passing.
Whether through tears or laughter, if that is your way of sending him off... then I'm sure he will gladly accept your sentiments.
Thank you... Seniora Prioress...
Allow me to accompany you around the flower fields outside.
Mister Ezell, that lady also said that her father 'passed away'...
Yes... People who pass away all come here.
People who are alive come to send them off... And after that, they carry on the wishes of the person who passed away, and continue on with life.
Then what about the people who passed away? What do they do?
Maybe... they're watching us from up high in the sky.
So we won't be able to see them again because they went into the sky?
Cecelia, this is for you.
This is... Mamma's guardian gun?
Yes.
My mama and papa were both executors for the Notarial Hall. I didn't see them much when I was little... and it was my nonno who raised me.
Nonno really loved his gun, and he'd tell me stories about his younger days every time he polished it.
He told me about the battles he fought, his friends, how he met my nonna, and the embarrassing things my papa did as a kid...
When I saw my nonno's gun, I would think back to the stories he told me.
Later, my nonno passed away. I was only a little older than you are now.
An executor from the Notarial Hall came and took away his guardian gun.
Just like how I was supposed to take away your mama's gun.
Ezell slowly crouches down and places Feoria's gun in Cecelia's hand.
She lowers her head and looks at her mother's patron firearm.
Ezell gently holds Cecelia's hand.
Warm drops of water fall onto the embossed floral pattern of the patron firearm's grip, collecting into a little pool in the young girl's palms.
Mister Ezell... Why am I crying...?
I watered the plants in the backyard every week, just like my nonno had taught me. When they blossomed, I was very happy and called for him to come and see.
But it was only when I called for him that I realized he wasn't there anymore.
I would never see my nonno again, or his gun.
Death is the last goodbye you'll ever say to someone.
But, to say goodbye is to overcome death.
For there to be a 'goodbye,' the living must move forward while the dead stay behind.
A 'goodbye' is the longing that the living have for the dead. It is proof that the dead lived.
And that is why we must say goodbye.
You look horrible. Did you pull an all-nighter?
He's back. I saw it, and it couldn't have been anyone else... That light... It was Andoain's Arts!
Ah... so that's why you vanished in a puff of smoke yesterday afternoon.
He came to you directly, though?
He wasn't there for me.
When I was bringing the mixed-blood girl back to the Basilica... I bumped into a Sarkaz.
A Sarkaz? Now, of all times? And in broad daylight on the streets of Laterano, too?
That's right... I went after the Sarkaz and told Ezell to take the girl back to the Basilica.
I chased the Sarkaz all the way to the edge of Pagus Faber... but Andoain covered her with his Arts and she got away.
As for Ezell, I thought he'd have made it to the Basilica by the evening.
But he never made it. This is his terminal. It was stuck to the bottom of a vehicle transporting envoys.
Heh, our trainee kid's a good enough actor to fool you?
I doubt he was acting. Though it's true that I don't understand why he did it... Maybe... Andoain kidnapped him, too...?
Fiammetta, I think you really ought to calm down a little.
I'm calm. I'm calmer than I've been in the last eight years.
But none of you ever told me what happened the day after I left.
It was a simple mop-up mission... and it went so much better than we expected...
All I did was answer a call for backup... I left the rest of you for four hours...
And by the time I got back to the ruins, it was all too late.
I've already told you everything I could.
What exactly did he get from the Lock and Key that gave him the nerve to do that to Lemuen?
I would tell you if I could... but I don't know myself.
Fiammetta, this is Laterano, the Lateran Basilica. We aren't running around ruins in the wilds of Kazdel anymore.
You need to get some rest, Fiammetta. How about I help you apply for some leave? If you don't use your annual leave, it just expires.
Are you serious?!
The conference is in two days, and Andoain brought a Sarkaz here to Laterano to kidnap a mixed-blood Sankta girl. He might have even attacked an executor...
I really let my guard down this time... The Sarkaz clearly knew her way around Pagus Faber. They must've infiltrated Laterano a long time ago... What is that maniac planning this time?!
I'm serious. Calm down.
The old man's already heard about the Astray, and so has Velliv. I was working in the Basilica yesterday too—Not that I like to think of meetings as work.

In any case, Laterano is really not in as much danger as you think. Andoain's just a Sankta. He's not a monster with six eyes and four arms. If you really want to take him on, go lie down for two hours.
We'll talk to Lemuen after that.
Looks like you're not completely out of it just yet.
Did you get enough rest last night?
I'm very well-rested. Thank you for taking us in, senior.
The Ecclesia Requietum's doors are always open, as long as you don't find it too depressing.
After all, both the living and the dead visit this place.
Given the circumstances, we're fortunate enough to have a roof over our heads. Speaking of which, where is Cecelia?
The girl you brought with you? The sisters told me that she slept very well last night. She must have been exhausted.
She's had a long day.
Mister Ezell, Mister Friar. Good morning.
Good morning, Cecelia. You're full of energy today.
Yeah... We have stuff to do.
I asked Mister Friar to help me with something.
He told me that... there is a ceremony for the family of the dead... to say their goodbyes.
I want to... say goodbye to Mamma...
We will hold a funeral for Miss Feoria.
I see... A funeral...
It's both a way to pay the dead our respects and for us to find closure.
I'll help you, Cecelia.
Allow me to thank you again, senior.
No, not at all. It is my honor, actually.
There is nothing more beautiful than finding the courage to take that step forward.
She is still young, and already she is saying farewell to a loved one. Not many grown men can handle this journey she is about to take.
May I come in?
Oh, it's you. At last.
Please, come in. She's here.
It's the singing cotton candy lady!
(Put your gun down. This is a heartfelt reunion.)
(It is no different from the countless emotional meetings that have taken place across the land.)
I knew it... It's you...
Cotton Candy Lady, I didn't get to talk to you yesterday.
I actually really, really wanted to! But I don't know why... everyone started to fight...
Your horns look just like Papà's. So pretty!
I wish I had horns like Papà too... I'm jealous.
Hehe... Really? But your halo is very pretty.
It's so bright that I sometimes can't sleep at night! It helps me read storybooks, though...
Cotton Candy Lady, are you from the same place as Papà? Why can't Papà live in the city? Why aren't there other Sarkaz in Laterano?
Oh... My name is Cecelia!
Hello, Cecelia. Call me... Rosella.
(Friar... Why is there a Sarkaz here?)
(Sarkaz... No Sarkaz have ever been allowed to set foot in our holy Lateran City.)
(But then, what is Cecelia?)
(Let's go. Give them some space.)
(What are you people trying to accomplish?)
(We're only... seeking our path forward.)
Good morning, Guide.
Salutations, Guide.
May God bless you and keep you, Guide.
They call you their guide?
I don't presume to be any sort of guide or leader. You may call me by my name, Andoain.
If being a leader helps my brothers and sisters feel at ease and points them in a direction, then I am willing to serve as their Guide. That's all.
I hope that's all it is.
I know what you're thinking.

The mastermind of a heretical cult, lurking in the shadows of Laterano to lead its citizens astray. A man plotting a shocking conspiracy during this important conference that the city spent so much time to prepare?
No, we wish only to throw open the doors.
To those who are rejected, harmed, insulted, and desecrated.
The Ecclesia Requietum will open its doors to them all.
But...
But both you and I are 'angels.' They are not. The sacred cathedrals and churches of the Sankta have no place for them.

Look at this old man. The Ursus hammering away at the coffin. He lost his family, his friends, his land, and his home during the war. What does he have to rely on, to believe in? Where can he find comfort and salvation?
After a long journey, he made his way here to Laterano, the land of peace and radiance he heard about from passing travelers.
But what did this place offer him? How did the glorious Lateran City treat this foreigner who had long been a toy of fate's cruelty?
See the Caprinae woman watering the herbs? She was branded a whore and a blasphemer for offending a noble, only barely escaping her home when it was set alight.

All she wanted was peace and quiet, or, if fate would allow her a bit of luxury, a brief moment of leisure and joy every now and then. Do you think the saints in the Basilica will think her their sister?

To these men and women, our glorious Lateran citizens might perhaps offer them a sympathetic smile, perhaps even passionately hear their woes, maybe even open up their wallets to show their kindness and virtue.
Yet, to the Sankta, all of this will always be somebody else's problem.
As for the Sarkaz, the 'cursed devils,' the 'crafty devils...' The 'devils' have never had a choice.

Our hatred of them has lasted thousands of years. For as long as history books go back, the Sankta and the Sarkaz have been slaughtering one another. The origin of this conflict is long gone. Our mutual hatred has become common sense.
Father and son slay father and son. Sisters murder sisters. The Sarkaz attack Sankta caravans for sport, while the Sankta shoot their Sarkaz enemies through the chest with greatest pride.
We are only ever concerned with how we see them, yet we are never interested in how they see us. Vengeance for vengeance grew into custom, and those customs eventually became tradition.
But, if you are willing to spend the night with them under the same roof, you will find that their joys and sorrows are no different from ours.

They rejoice when reunited, they weep when one passes, they tease their friends, and they grow bashful when they fall in love. Some of them love drink, others love song. Some of them even choose to dedicate their lives to their faith.
How are they any different from us?

In Laterano, even the most devout Liberi friars who carefully follow the Church's teachings, who dutifully carry out their tasks in each ceremony, even those who are made saints and created cardinals...
The Church has never truly accepted them. The Liberi are only there to lend a helping hand. They are tools to be used in the Church's day-to-day affairs.
They don't have halos, which means they... heh, they aren't 'chosen,' they aren't Sankta.
Laterano is a place of peace and beauty, a place full of joy and laughter, but only we are deserving of this 'grace.'
What you're saying... is apostasy.
Young executor, let me ask you. Why—
Why are the Sankta the only ones allowed happiness?
You know who Cecelia is...
We've known for years. She's the child of a Sankta and a Sarkaz. If grace actually existed, she would be it.
So you're trying to kidnap Cecelia now because of the conference?
We are not concerned with the conference at all.
We came to take Cecelia now because, as a child, she should be by her mother's side.
As soon as we are done with what we came here for, we will leave Laterano.
What are you trying to accomplish?
You seem very tense. First things first, we must hold a funeral for Cecelia's mother.
Do you see the place up ahead with all the flowers? That's where the gravesite is. Tomorrow, that's where Feoria will be buried.
If all are equal when they pass—
Then so should they be when they live.
Come out.
Executor Federico.

Your Excellency, if I may ask, did you provide the Sarkaz with a path to infiltrate Laterano? If indeed you did, I would be required to take you in to the relevant Tribunal of the Lateran Curia for questioning.
Must we make things so difficult between us?
I suggest you cooperate with the Notarial Hall. Should you resist arrest, regulations stipulate that I can and will temporarily suspend your rights as a Lateran citizen.
How exactly do you explain this encounter of ours with canon laws and teachings?
Should we interpret it as a test, or does it mean we have lost our way?
Or perhaps... losing our way is part of the test?
It is clear that you are resisting arrest.
Unfortunately, this encounter will not be resolved peacefully.
Tell me more!
Oh, be careful with the candle! Don't get burnt!
Ah! Can't mess up the shape... It won't look pretty.
I need to... make it look nicer... for Mamma.
Alright, I've told you a lot already. It's all boring adventures and the same old day-to-day life. As for Kazdel in the legends...
It's kind of embarrassing to say, but none of us have ever been.
Kazdel is to the Sarkaz... like what Laterano is to the Sankta, right? It's the Sarkaz homeland...
Everyone's gotta be very happy. There are cotton candy food trucks, right? And candy stores, too... There are lots of happy Sarkaz on the streets, all of them saying hi to each other!
Maybe...
It could be like that, one day.
Why don't we talk about you instead, Cecelia? What are your papa and mama like?
I... I don't actually know...
I haven't seen Papà that many times... Every time we went to see him, Mamma did a lot of work to prepare, and we always left at night... We walked very far... to the forest.
We met him in the forest, and he wore a big cape, like he hurried to us from somewhere far away...
I don't actually remember what he looks like very well, but I remember his horns! They're black and straight, and they twinkle bright under the moonlight!

He took me by my hand and walked around the forest. He told me the names of the trees and how old they were... It gets very quiet at night outside the city. You don't even hear fowlbeasts. But I was never scared.
He grabbed my hand so tight. It kinda hurt... but I didn't say anything.
Every time we saw each other, he looked like he was even happier than I was! And he also looked... a little nervous?

When I got tired, Mamma would carry me on her back and take me home. When I looked back, Papà would be there under the big tree. He kept looking at us. Even after we got very far away, he would still stand there...
Your mama and papa are both wonderful people.
Mamma's amazing! She knows how to do everything!
Look. This is Mamma's guardian gun! Ezell gave it to me.
Mamma said that Papà couldn't beat her in a fight if he tried... She always let Papà win! But I know Papà's really amazing, too!
Haha, Cecelia. When I say 'wonderful,' it probably doesn't mean the same thing as what you mean by 'amazing.'
Really? I'm sure Papà and Mamma are both wonderful and amazing!
Mamma never told me how she met Papà, though. Every time I asked her, she would start talking about something else... Why didn't she want to tell me?

When you're still a kid, there are a lot of other, more important things. Do you like watching tea brew? When I was your age, I loved looking at brewing tea. The water would get darker and darker by the second...
Rosella... The way you're talking about something else now is just like Mamma...
I may look like I don't know a lot, but actually... I know a little.
I ran all around the city yesterday, but I didn't see any Sarkaz besides you... Sarkaz aren't supposed to come to Laterano, are they?
That's why Papà and Mamma shouldn't be together... No, I mean, there is somebody who doesn't want him to be with Mamma, right?
No, there's no such thing as two people who shouldn't be together... All this will change. And it won't be too long from now... It will happen.
Love isn't a crime.
I understand...
That's why we are here.
And when that day comes, can I walk around Laterano all I want?
Of course.
I won't have to hide in my room anymore? I can visit all the candy stores in Laterano one at a time?
Just once? You can go as many times as you want.
Wow... I want that day to come soon... I wanna grow up soon!
Right, maybe if you grow up soon, it'll just...
No, Cecelia, there's no hurry. When I was your age, I also thought that growing up would solve everything, but when I finally grew up...
Growing up is very, very, very tough, Cecelia, and it will be even harder for you. The burden that you'll carry on your shoulders... No, never mind. You're still a kid. Let's not think about that.
Even though everyone grows up?
Right... We don't have a choice but to grow up.
Rosella, who has been smiling the whole time, suddenly seems somewhat lonely, all of a sudden.
She looks out the chapel's window at the graveyard outside. It's full of blooming flowers, and in the distance, the tall statue in the center of Lateran City makes a faint impression.
She starts humming a song, sorrowful but with a hint of hope.
Oh, that song! You hummed it yesterday too...
When Mamma taught me that song, she said she didn't know the words anymore, so she always just sang it with lalalas, but I really like how it sounds... What is the song actually about?

This is a Sarkaz song. It's about a hero who's about to go on a journey to do something really amazing, and that's why everyone sang this song to say their goodbyes and send him on his way, hoping he'll come back safely.
The hero of the song is going somewhere far away to fulfill his dreams...
It may be a long, long journey, but he will surely, certainly...
Mister Ezell! Mister Friar!
What are you chatting about?
Good day, Guide—Nothing important. Just old stories about the Sarkaz and a few trivial topics.
I'm having fun... I've never talked to someone so long.
Mister Ezell, did you pick these flowers?
Yes, I thought... we could extract the flower's fragrance and give the candles a little scent.
Fragrance can help guide the departed in the right direction.
That's so nice! Thanks, Mister Ezell!
Um... Actually... I'm... I'm a little scared.
When I say goodbye to Mamma at the funeral... That's going to be the last goodbye, right?
Does that really mean I can't see her anymore? Not even on holidays? Not even once in a long while, like Papà? Even... if I just want her to pat my head?
Even... Even if...
There's still a story about an angel and a burdenbeast... that she hasn't finished telling me?
Cecelia...
Tonight's the last time, Cecelia.
I'll stay with you in your mama's room tonight, okay? All of us will be there. You won't be alone.
A Sarkaz watching over a Sankta child and her mother...
The candlelight may be feeble, but it too can light up the path to the other side.
Lemuen, we're here to see you.
We brought you some dessert.
A fruit tart I swiped from the Basilica.
Welcome. By the way, that cactus looks disgusting.
That's our girl, Lemuen. You've got fine eyes.
Do you two really miss me so much that you have to visit every day?
We could come see you every hour, if that's what you want.
Trying to curry favor, are you, Mostima? And you, Fiammetta... That's a terrible smile if I've ever seen one.
Did something happen?
It's Andoain...
You ran into him.
We encountered a Sarkaz who had infiltrated Laterano, and when I was chasing her... I saw his Originium Arts firsthand.
I see. It's good that you aren't hurt.
Lemuen, are you sure you're okay here? This is a normal hospital. If he...
He's paid me a visit already.
What did you just—
*Sigh*...
A silence descends upon the room.
Lemuen looks at the flowers on the table.
The way you two talk about him is like... you're talking about a picnic you had over the weekend.
Lemuen, I'm not mad about you...
But, whenever we talk about him, you... and Mostima too. Each and every time, whenever I bring it up, you two always have that same look.
Whenever I see that expression on your faces, it makes me think... maybe it wasn't him who went crazy that day. That I lost my mind.
We felt it, is all...
Your Sankta hive mind again? Fine, you felt what he felt. Why did you point your gun at him, then?
That has nothing to do with this. It didn't matter how... out of it he was, I couldn't let him take the Lock and Key.
What about after that? You fell because of him! Does that not bother you?
Lemuen, it was his Arts that put you in this hospital. You fell into a coma for five whole years... does that not bother you?
Fiammetta...
Trust me, I'm on your side.
It's just that I could really feel...
I don't want to hear that word anymore.
Do you think I'm mad about all this for no good reason?
Of course not, I understand...
Right, of course. All of you understand.
I'm the only one who doesn't.
Hey, Fiammetta!
*Sigh*...
Fiammetta, Fiammetta!
Shimmering Vigil!
You want me to bash your face in?!
Listen to me!
I've got something to say to you, Mostima!
I'm done with you stinking Sankta already! Your empathy? The way you understand each other? I don't want to hear any of it!
Did those halos fry your angel brains? You and Lemuen almost died in those ruins!
Why aren't you angry? Why aren't you mad? Why am I the only one... the only one who's mad?
Don't give me that arrogant smile and that 'nothing matters' attitude! You had to leave Laterano and drift all across the world, bum out in the boonies. Whose fault do you think that was?!
I followed you all the way to Victoria, to Leithanien, Sargon, Yan...
What do you think that was for?!
You, Lemuen, Andoain... our team. There was a time... when we trusted each other.
What kind of God Almighty reason does he have anyway? What gave him the right to betray our trust? And what gave you two the right to understand him? Why should I have to understand him?!

Do you know how much I miss those days? All those times we finished a mission after all those close calls... all those times Lemuen took us out to drinks... all those times we tried out a new dessert shop...
Even those times he fixed our reports...
What gave him the right to ruin it all?!
Those days...
Those days... mean a lot to me...
I understand...
Fi...
Lateran irises.
They grow only in the Central Garden of Saint Marcel, the Sunset Chapel, and the Ecclesia Requietum in Stevonus.
Yeah.
Come on, we'll go together.
You're my warden. Better make sure I'm not hurt on the job.
Hm? How deep are we?
Reckon we should dig a little more?
It'll all be a wasteland here sooner or later. It's not like you could stick wheels under the graves.
Folks want to leave behind some trace of their lives after they die... but it's all a pointless dream, sadly. Might as well ask for a cremation and have your ashes scattered in the wilderness.
That would put us out of a job, though.
True enough.
If you ask me, graves aren't for the dead. It's the living who need these things... The ceremonies and the big chunk of rock that goes on top of them.
Those are the things that folks say their goodbyes to before starting anew—
Hah, until it's their turn to bite the dust.
The preparations for the ritual are almost complete.
Um...
I... I'm not sure.
I... I don't feel so good today...
Mamma...
Mister Friar, I was really happy yesterday...
Rosella told me about lots of fun stuff... We prepared for the funeral together, and it felt like... I was doing something for Mamma.
But... we're done with everything now... What comes next?
I think... I worked really hard... for Mamma...
But... can Mamma see all that?
Does all of this... really mean something?
Mister Friar, have you gone through this a lot?
When it happens... during... the funeral... what... what should I do?
And what do I do... once it's over?
Hm. Right, the funeral...
You're still young, but I don't want to hide anything from you.
We do a lot of things for others, but, in the end, we do it for ourselves.
Cecelia, this isn't selfishness. Rather, it just means that, ultimately, everything that the living do is to mold and shape themselves.
We hold funerals and say our goodbyes when our loved ones leave us, but it's also a respite for ourselves.
It's a way to mark the road that we've trodden.
Many grown-ups... use this as a way to find peace, but whether they truly find it, or indeed anything, isn't the fault of the funeral itself.
It's really up to us, after we go through the thing.
Once we are done looking back and resting, we must keep going forward.
But where do we go after that?
That is something you need to ask yourself, Cecelia.
This is a question that only you can answer yourself, and it's a question that both you, I, and everyone must answer in our lifetimes.
Ask myself...
Only you can find the right way forward for yourself, and it is only with your own two legs that you can walk that path.
What about you, Mister Friar? You know so much... What is your road like?
I can't answer you that. I'm afraid I can't even say I've found it.
It's a tough search, and a sad journey. It makes you wonder if the way forward ever truly exists.
But I haven't given up, and that is why I, and my brothers and sisters, call ourselves...
...'the Pathfinders.'
Candelabra, candelabra. Where does one find...?
(Somebody's there... That Liberi from the hospital? Patia, was it? And the Legatus called Oren, too.
I really don't get it. What is that guy even doing?
Watch your words, Oren.
This is the perfect time to act. There are lots of other things we could be doing besides wasting time on a funeral.
And I thought you were close to the girl's mother. How cold-blooded of you.
I'd already made my decision when I told Andoain about Cecelia.
She's capable of knocking this city off its godly throne.
That also means she can shake that saint who's always hiding behind the red and gold curtain... and plenty of others besides.
(What is he talking about?)
A Sankta wants to bring down his holy city with the help of a mixed-blood girl? Scandalous.
You make it sound like I just want to burn it all down for no good reason. I said 'capable.' That doesn't mean we have to do it.
In fact, if we don't do it, then her worth as a bargaining chip is that much more.
I just have to make sure Cecelia doesn't end up in the Pope's hands... On that point at least, Andoain and I are aligned.
A word of advice.
I don't know what kind of tricks you're trying to play with Cecelia, but do not forget the promise you made to the Guide.
Thanks for the tip... I don't mind walking this path with you folks for a little longer.
Now, you'll have to excuse me. I have better places to be than a funeral.
(Looks like they aren't all on the same wavelength.)
(But what worries me more... is that there's a Legatus involved.)
(I'll take Cecelia and leave once the funeral is over.)
Cecelia. Cecelia!
Senior Ezell, the funeral will be starting soon.
Andoain, I don't care about your grand ideals, and I'm not interested in discussing faith with you.
So once the funeral is over... I'm leaving with Cecelia.
Where will you go?
None of your concern.
It's time to light the candles, Senior Ezell. Go, be with Cecelia.
The early spring mornings are still frigid here in Laterano.
Sankta funerals are often accompanied by laughter, music, sweets, and the occasional, inconsequential explosion.
The departed become one with the Holy Spirit and return to Heaven. This is worthy of celebration.
But there is another kind of Sankta 'funeral' unknown to other races:
On a certain hour of a certain day, a great sorrow will seize all Sankta by the throat. They will not be able to move, breathe, or avert their eyes.
Each and every fragile soul will be swept away as if by the floodwaters of a broken dam.
Yet neither the joy of the former nor the awesome power of the latter can be found today in the Ecclesia Requietum of Pagus Stevonus.
We find only a frail girl, trying her hardest to swing the absurdly unwieldy shovel in her hands.
A girl trying to shovel the last bit of dirt onto her mother's resting place.
Go, Cecelia. It's time to see goodbye.
